text:
{
  "english": {
    "IsA": [
      "It is a self-governing Caribbean archipelago and island organized as an unincorporated territory of the United States under the designation of commonwealth.",
      "It is classified by the International Monetary Fund as a developed jurisdiction."
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "It has approximately 3.2 million people.",
      "It has 78 municipalities.",
      "It has a nonvoting representative to the U.S. Congress.",
      "It has a territorial constitution approved by Congress and Puerto Rican voters in 1952.",
      "It has a financial oversight board to oversee debt restructuring."
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "It is characterized by Spanish and English as official languages of the government, though Spanish predominates.",
      "It is characterized by being a stepping-stone in the passage from Europe to Cuba, Mexico, Central America, and the northern territories of South America.",
      "It is characterized by being one of five territories with less representation in the federal government.",
      "It is characterized by a two-question referendum in 2012 on its political status.",
      "It is characterized by suffering back-to-back large hurricanes in 2017: Hurricane Irma and Hurricane Maria."
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "It is used for manufacturing, primarily pharmaceuticals, petrochemicals, and electronics.",
      "It is used for tourism and hospitality services.",
      "It is used as a stepping-stone in the passage from Europe to Cuba, Mexico, Central America, and the northern territories of South America.",
      "It is used for economic projects to develop into an industrial high-income economy.",
      "It is used for debt restructuring in response to the Puerto Rican government-debt crisis."
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "It is located between the Dominican Republic in the Greater Antilles and the U.S. Virgin Islands in the Lesser Antilles.",
      "It is located about 1,000 miles southeast of Miami, Florida."
    ],
    "BuiltIn": [
      "It was built by the U.S. government and the Puerto Rico Industrial Development Company starting in the mid-20th century."
    ],
    "BuiltBy": [
      "It was built by the U.S. government and the Puerto Rico Industrial Development Company starting in the mid-20th century."
    ],
    "OriginatedIn": [
      "It originated in the settlement of the Ortoiroid people before 430 BC."
    ],
    "FoundedBy": [
      "It was founded by Juan Ponce de León in 1508."
    ],
    "FoundedIn": [
      "It was founded in 1508."
    ],
    "CreatedBy": [
      "It was created by a constitutional assembly on July 25, 1952."
    ],
    "CreatedIn": [
      "It was created in 1952."
    ],
    "DevelopedBy": [
      "It was developed by the U.S. government together with the Puerto Rico Industrial Development Company."
    ],
    "DevelopedIn": [
      "It was developed in the mid-20th century."
    ],
    "DiscoveredBy": [
      "It was discovered by Christopher Columbus in 1493."
    ],
    "DiscoveredIn": [
      "It was discovered in 1493."
    ],
    "InventedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],

    ],
    "ProducedBy": [

    ],
    "CausedBy": [
      "It is caused by new infectious diseases carried by Europeans, exploitation by Spanish settlers, and warfare.",
      "It is caused by the Puerto Rican government-debt crisis.",
      "It is caused by back-to-back large hurricanes in 2017: Hurricane Irma and Hurricane Maria."
    ],
    "Affects": [
      "It affects the U.S. Constitution in areas such as foreign affairs or defense.",
      "It affects the U.S. Congress, which oversees it under the Puerto Rico Federal Relations Act of 1950.",
      "It affects federal elections, as residents are disenfranchised.",
      "It affects federal taxes, as residents pay them.",
      "It affects personal income tax, as most residents are exempt from federal income tax on personal income earned in Puerto Rico."
    ],
    "ConsistsOf": [
      "It consists of the eponymous main island and numerous smaller islands, including Vieques, Culebra, and Mona."
    ],
    "PartOf": [
      "It is part of the Greater Antilles.",
      "It is part of the Spanish Empire.",
      "It is part of the New World.",
      "It is part of the United States as an unincorporated territory."
    ],
    "MemberOf": [
      "It is a member of the United Nations Special Committee on Decolonization."
    ],
    "BelongsTo": [
      "It belongs to the United States."
    ],
    "KnownFor": [
      "It is known for its distinct Puerto Rican identity, centered on a fusion of European, African, and Indigenous elements.",
      "It is known for its political status being an ongoing debate.",
      "It is known for its economic sectors of manufacturing, primarily pharmaceuticals, petrochemicals, and electronics, followed by services, namely tourism and hospitality.",
      "It is known as Borinquen, a derivation of Borikén, its indigenous Taíno name.",
      "It is known in Spanish as La Isla del Encanto, meaning 'the island of enchantment'."
    ],
    "AssociatedWith": [

      "It is associated with the Spanish Empire.",
      "It is associated with the Spanish–American War.",
      "It is associated with the Jones–Shafroth Act of 1917.",
      "It is associated with the Puerto Rico Oversight, Management, and Economic Stability Act of 2016."
    ],

      "It is named after Christopher Columbus."

      "It was held in the New World.",
      "It was held in the United States."

      "It is about the history of Puerto Rico.",
      "It is about the economy of Puerto Rico.",
      "It is about the culture of Puerto Rico.",
      "It is about the relationship between Puerto Rico and the United States."

      "It was influenced by Spain.",
      "It was influenced by European powers.",
      "It was influenced by African slaves.",
      "It was influenced by the United States."

  "japanese": {
    "IsA": [
      "それは自治的なカリブ海の群島であり、連邦指定の下でアメリカ合衆国の未編入領土として組織化された島です。",
      "それは国際通貨基金によって先進国として分類されています。"
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "それは約320万人の人々を抱えています。",
      "それは78の自治体を持っています。",
      "それはアメリカ合衆国連邦議会に一人の投票権のない代表を送っています。",
      "それは1952年に連邦議会とプエルトリコ有権者によって承認された領土憲法を持っています。",
      "それは債務再編を監督するための財政監督委員会を持っています。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それは政府の公用語としてスペイン語と英語を特徴としていますが、スペイン語が優勢です。",
      "それはヨーロッパからキューバ、メキシコ、中央アメリカ、南アメリカ北部領土への通過点であったことを特徴としています。",
      "それは連邦政府における代表が少ない5つの領土の一つであることを特徴としています。",
      "それは2012年の政治的地位に関する二項投票を特徴としています。",
      "それは2017年にカテゴリー5のハリケーン・イルマとカテゴリー4のハリケーン・マリアという連続した大型ハリケーンに見舞われたことを特徴としています。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それは製造業、主に製薬、石油化学、電子機器に使用されています。",
      "それは観光およびホスピタリティサービスに使用されています。",
      "それはヨーロッパからキューバ、メキシコ、中央アメリカ、南アメリカ北部領土への通過点として使用されています。",
      "それは産業的な高所得経済へと発展させるための経済プロジェクトに使用されています。",
      "それはプエルトリコ政府債務危機に対応した債務再編に使用されています。"
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "それは大アンティル諸島にあるドミニカ共和国と小アンティル諸島にあるアメリカ領ヴァージン諸島の間に位置しています。",
      "それはフロリダ州マイアミから約1,000マイル南東に位置しています。"
    ],
    "BuiltIn": [
      "それは1950年代半ばにアメリカ合衆国政府とプエルトリコ産業開発公社によって建設されました。"
    ],
    "BuiltBy": [
      "それは1950年代半ばにアメリカ合衆国政府とプエルトリコ産業開発公社によって建設されました。"
    ],
    "OriginatedIn": [
      "それは紀元前430年以前のオルイトイド人の入植に起源があります。"
    ],
    "FoundedBy": [
      "それは1508年にフアン・ポンセ・デ・レオンによって設立されました。"
    ],
    "FoundedIn": [
      "それは1508年に設立されました。"
    ],
    "CreatedBy": [
      "それは1952年7月25日に憲法制定議会によって作成されました。"
    ],
    "CreatedIn": [
      "それは1952年に作成されました。"
    ],
    "DevelopedBy": [
      "それはアメリカ合衆国政府とプエルトリコ産業開発公社によって開発されました。"
    ],
    "DevelopedIn": [
      "それは1950年代半ばに開発されました。"
    ],
    "DiscoveredBy": [
      "それは1493年にクリストファー・コロンブスによって発見されました。"
    ],
    "DiscoveredIn": [
      "それは1493年に発見されました。"
    ],
    "InventedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "InventedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "PublishedIn": [
      "それはヨーロッパからの入植者を惹きつけるために、スペイン語、英語、フランス語で出版されました。"
    ],
    "WrittenBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "ComposedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "DirectedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "PerformedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "ManufacturedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "ProducedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "CausedBy": [
      "それはヨーロッパ人によって持ち込まれた新しい感染症、スペイン植民者による搾取、そして戦争によって引き起こされました。",
      "それはプエルトリコ政府債務危機によって引き起こされました。",
      "それは2017年のハリケーン・イルマとハリケーン・マリアという連続した大型ハリケーンによって引き起こされました。"
    ],
    "Affects": [
      "それは外交や防衛などの分野でアメリカ合衆国憲法に影響を与えます。",
      "それはプエルトリコ連邦関係法1950に基づきそれを監督するアメリカ合衆国連邦議会に影響を与えます。",
      "それは住民が選挙権を剥奪されているため、連邦選挙に影響を与えます。",
      "それは住民がそれらを支払うため、連邦税に影響を与えます。",
      "それはほとんどの住民がプエルトリコで稼いだ個人所得に対する連邦所得税を免除されているため、個人所得税に影響を与えます。"
    ],
    "ConsistsOf": [
      "それは同名の主要な島と、ビエケス島、クレブラ島、モナ島を含む多数の小さな島々で構成されています。"
    ],
    "PartOf": [
      "それは大アンティル諸島の一部です。",
      "それはスペイン帝国の一部です。",
      "それは新世界の一部です。",
      "それはアメリカ合衆国の未編入領土の一部です。"
    ],
    "MemberOf": [
      "それは国連脱植民地化特別委員会の一員です。"
    ],
    "BelongsTo": [
      "それはアメリカ合衆国に属しています。"
    ],
    "KnownFor": [
      "それはヨーロッパ、アフリカ、先住民の要素の融合を中心とした、独特のプエルトリコ人のアイデンティティで知られています。",
      "それは政治的地位が継続的な議論の対象となっていることで知られています。",
      "それは製造業、主に製薬、石油化学、電子機器、それに続くサービス業、すなわち観光とホスピタリティの経済部門で知られています。",
      "それはその先住民タイノ族の名前であるボリケンから派生したボリンケンとして知られています。",
      "それはスペイン語で「魔法の島」を意味するラ・イスラ・デル・エンカントとして知られています。"
    ],
    "AssociatedWith": [
      "それは連邦指定と関連付けられています。",
      "それはスペイン帝国と関連付けられています。",
      "それは米西戦争と関連付けられています。",
      "それは1917年のジョーンズ・シャフラス法と関連付けられています。",
      "それは2016年のプエルトリコ監督管理経済安定法と関連付けられています。"
    ],
    "NamedAfter": [
      "それは聖ヨハネ・バプテスマにちなんで名付けられました。",
      "それはクリストファー・コロンブスにちなんで名付けられました。"
    ],
    "OccurredIn": [
      "それは北東カリブ海で発生しました。",
      "それは新世界で発生しました。",
      "それは16世紀、17世紀、18世紀に発生しました。",
      "それは19世紀に発生しました。",
      "それは1898年に発生しました。"
    ],
    "HeldIn": [
      "それはスペイン帝国で保持されていました。",
      "それは新世界で保持されていました。",
      "それはアメリカ合衆国で保持されていました。"
    ],
    "WonBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "About": [
      "それはプエルトリコの政治的地位についてです。",
      "それはプエルトリコの歴史についてです。",
      "それはプエルトリコの経済についてです。",
      "それはプエルトリコの文化についてです。",
      "それはプエルトリコとアメリカ合衆国の関係についてです。"
    ],
    "InfluencedBy": [
      "それはアメリカ大陸の先住民によって影響を受けました。",
      "それはスペインによって影響を受けました。",
      "それはヨーロッパの列強によって影響を受けました。",
      "それはアフリカの奴隷によって影響を受けました。",
      "それはアメリカ合衆国によって影響を受けました。"
    ],
    "Influenced": [
      "それは文化と人口統計の状況に影響を与えました。",
      "それは政治的状況に影響を与えました。",
      "それは経済的状況に影響を与えました。",
      "それは人々のアイデンティティに影響を与えました。",
      "それはカリブ海地域の歴史に影響を与えました。"
    ]
  }
}

IsA: It is a self-governing Caribbean archipelago and island organized as an unincorporated territory of the United States under the designation of commonwealth.
It is classified by the International Monetary Fund as a developed jurisdiction.
HasA: It has a nonvoting representative to the U.S. Congress.
It has a territorial constitution approved by Congress and Puerto Rican voters in 1952.
It has a financial oversight board to oversee debt restructuring.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by Spanish and English as official languages of the government, though Spanish predominates.
It is characterized by being a stepping-stone in the passage from Europe to Cuba, Mexico, Central America, and the northern territories of South America.
It is characterized by being one of five territories with less representation in the federal government.
It is characterized by a two-question referendum in 2012 on its political status.
It is characterized by suffering back-to-back large hurricanes in 2017: Hurricane Irma and Hurricane Maria.
UsedFor: It is used for manufacturing, primarily pharmaceuticals, petrochemicals, and electronics.
It is used for tourism and hospitality services.
It is used as a stepping-stone in the passage from Europe to Cuba, Mexico, Central America, and the northern territories of South America.
It is used for economic projects to develop into an industrial high-income economy.
It is used for debt restructuring in response to the Puerto Rican government-debt crisis.
LocatedIn: It is located between the Dominican Republic in the Greater Antilles and the U.S. Virgin Islands in the Lesser Antilles.
It is located about 1,000 miles southeast of Miami, Florida.
BuiltIn: It was built by the U.S. government and the Puerto Rico Industrial Development Company starting in the mid-20th century.
BuiltBy: It was built by the U.S. government and the Puerto Rico Industrial Development Company starting in the mid-20th century.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the settlement of the Ortoiroid people before 430 BC.
FoundedBy: It was founded by Juan Ponce de León in 1508.
CreatedBy: It was created by a constitutional assembly on July 25, 1952.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by the U.S. government together with the Puerto Rico Industrial Development Company.
DevelopedIn: It was developed in the mid-20th century.
DiscoveredBy: It was discovered by Christopher Columbus in 1493.
PublishedIn: It was published in Spanish, English and French in order to attract Europeans.
CausedBy: It is caused by new infectious diseases carried by Europeans, exploitation by Spanish settlers, and warfare.
It is caused by the Puerto Rican government-debt crisis.
It is caused by back-to-back large hurricanes in 2017: Hurricane Irma and Hurricane Maria.
Affects: It affects the U.S. Constitution in areas such as foreign affairs or defense.
It affects the U.S. Congress, which oversees it under the Puerto Rico Federal Relations Act of 1950.
It affects federal elections, as residents are disenfranchised.
It affects federal taxes, as residents pay them.
It affects personal income tax, as most residents are exempt from federal income tax on personal income earned in Puerto Rico.
ConsistsOf: It consists of the eponymous main island and numerous smaller islands, including Vieques, Culebra, and Mona.
PartOf: It is part of the Greater Antilles.
It is part of the Spanish Empire.
It is part of the New World.
It is part of the United States as an unincorporated territory.
MemberOf: It is a member of the United Nations Special Committee on Decolonization.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the United States.
KnownFor: It is known for its distinct Puerto Rican identity, centered on a fusion of European, African, and Indigenous elements.
It is known for its political status being an ongoing debate.
It is known for its economic sectors of manufacturing, primarily pharmaceuticals, petrochemicals, and electronics, followed by services, namely tourism and hospitality.
It is known as Borinquen, a derivation of Borikén, its indigenous Taíno name.
It is known in Spanish as La Isla del Encanto, meaning 'the island of enchantment'.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the designation of commonwealth.
It is associated with the Spanish Empire.
It is associated with the Spanish–American War.
It is associated with the Jones–Shafroth Act of 1917.
It is associated with the Puerto Rico Oversight, Management, and Economic Stability Act of 2016.
NamedAfter: It is named after Saint John the Baptist.
It is named after Christopher Columbus.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the northeastern Caribbean.
It occurred in the New World.
It occurred in the 16th, 17th and 18th centuries.
HeldIn: It was held in the Spanish Empire.
It was held in the New World.
It was held in the United States.
About: It is about the political status of Puerto Rico.
It is about the history of Puerto Rico.
It is about the economy of Puerto Rico.
It is about the culture of Puerto Rico.
It is about the relationship between Puerto Rico and the United States.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Indigenous peoples of the Americas.
It was influenced by European powers.
It was influenced by African slaves.
It was influenced by the United States.
Influenced: It influenced the cultural and demographic landscape.
It influenced the identity of its people.
It influenced the history of the Caribbean region.

Affects: それは外交や防衛などの分野でアメリカ合衆国憲法に影響を与えます。
それはプエルトリコ連邦関係法1950に基づきそれを監督するアメリカ合衆国連邦議会に影響を与えます。
それは住民が選挙権を剥奪されているため、連邦選挙に影響を与えます。
それは住民がそれらを支払うため、連邦税に影響を与えます。
それはほとんどの住民がプエルトリコで稼いだ個人所得に対する連邦所得税を免除されているため、個人所得税に影響を与えます。
ConsistsOf: それは同名の主要な島と、ビエケス島、クレブラ島、モナ島を含む多数の小さな島々で構成されています。

MemberOf: それは国連脱植民地化特別委員会の一員です。

KnownFor: それはヨーロッパ、アフリカ、先住民の要素の融合を中心とした、独特のプエルトリコ人のアイデンティティで知られています。
それは政治的地位が継続的な議論の対象となっていることで知られています。
それは製造業、主に製薬、石油化学、電子機器、それに続くサービス業、すなわち観光とホスピタリティの経済部門で知られています。
それはその先住民タイノ族の名前であるボリケンから派生したボリンケンとして知られています。
それはスペイン語で「魔法の島」を意味するラ・イスラ・デル・エンカントとして知られています。
